specialty: AR (mechanical) — primary; explicit Auto Body / Collision Repair vertical (industries/auto-body-shop/ and industries/collision-repair page) but mechanical AR is the dominant book of business. Treat as Both, AR-leaning.
base_monthly_high_usd: [uncertain] — not published. Third-party comparison estimates $2,000–$5,000+/mo for full RPM program; top end likely higher for multi-location.
typical_monthly_usd: [uncertain] ~$1,500–$3,500/mo for a single-location independent shop on the full RPM program (website + SEO + PPC + social + account mgmt). Per Service Stories' public comparison, 'typically $2,000–$5,000+/month based on industry standards.'
setup_fee_usd: Partner/promo pricing: $1 setup via AutoZone, ATI, and Turnkey Marketing partner channels. Standard setup [uncertain] but typically rolled into first month.
min_ad_spend_usd: [uncertain] — not published. PPC is included in RPM, ad spend billed separately on top of management fee.
contract_length_months: Month-to-month (no annual contract). Per Terms: 'Because there's no annual contract, your rate is subject to change, but we'll always notify you beforehand.'
cancellation_policy: 5-day full refund window from order date. After 5 days payments are non-refundable; service continues until end of current billing period. Cancel anytime via Manage Account page or by contacting Autoshop Solutions. Social Media and Paid Search (PPC) payments are non-refundable. Late fee 1.5%/mo on past-due balances. Termination on default: 14 business days to cure.
asset_ownership: [uncertain on full transfer rights] Hosting is on Autoshop Solutions' dedicated servers and bundled in monthly fee — implies website is on their stack and migration off is non-trivial. Terms state 'all grants of any license to use or transfer ownership of any intellectual property rights under this Agreement are conditioned on full payment' — i.e., client receives IP rights only after settling all balances, and even then transfer mechanics are not spelled out publicly. Client retains rights to content/photos/trademarks they supply.
pricing_transparency: Quote-only for full RPM. One published entry price: $179/mo via AutoZone-partner channel. Tiered structure ('Regardless of the level you choose') but tier prices not on website — uses a 'Right-Fit' calculator that asks weekly car count, ARO, and revenue, then quotes a recommended budget.
known_pain_points: Website + hosting locked to their proprietary platform (autoshopdashboard.com analytics, mystagingwebsite.com staging domain) — migration off is friction-heavy
PPC and Social payments non-refundable even on cancellation within month
Glassdoor employee reviews mention management/culture concerns (3.6/5 across 37 reviews)
Pricing opacity for full RPM program; only the partner-tier $179 entry SKU is published
notable_clients: Xtreme Auto Collision Repair & Spa (Raleigh, NC)
pros: Largest and most-decorated independent agency in the AR niche — Inc 5000 (2013/14/15), multiple HERMES Platinum and AVA Digital awards
Deep partner ecosystem: AutoZone, ATI, AASP, ASTA, CCA, Shop-Ware, Protractor, AutoServe1, Turnkey Marketing, RLO Training, 360 Payments, NCS-SSI
Month-to-month — no annual lock-in (genuine differentiator vs Hibu)
Explicit collision/auto-body vertical with case studies (Towne & Country, Xtreme Auto Collision)
Published entry-tier pricing via AutoZone partnership at $179/mo
cons: Website hosted on their dedicated platform — asset portability is a real concern on exit
Full RPM pricing not transparent — requires sales call and 'Right-Fit' qualification
Employee Glassdoor 3.6/5 — some culture complaints could affect account-team continuity
Estimated revenue ~$611K (ZoomInfo) appears low for a 75-employee company — data may be stale or wrong, treat with caution
koffman_relevance: Strongest single replacement candidate for Hibu. Month-to-month + 5-day refund directly attacks Hibu's biggest pain point (6-month contracts and cancellation friction documented across BBB, Trustpilot, JustAnswer). Has explicit collision/auto-body vertical that SMP lacks publicly, and is the named preferred marketing provider for AASP and CCA — relevant credentialing for a WI shop. Already a partner of Turnkey Marketing, so it can either replace Turnkey or coexist (Turnkey handles direct-mail/retention; ASS handles digital + website). For a single-location WI AR + collision shop like Koffman, plausible spend is ~$1,500–$3,500/mo all-in on RPM, plus media spend. Real watch-outs: (1) website is locked to their hosting stack — negotiate explicit asset/domain/content transfer terms upfront and (2) verify Wisconsin / Midwest case studies (most named clients are TN/NC/East Coast). Net: replaces Hibu cleanly, can replace Turnkey for direct-response digital but not for direct-mail retention loops.
